raw_text.question: Determine the seating capacity of a lecture hall if there are 32 rows of seats, with 18 seats in the first row, 21 seats in the second row, 24 seats in the third row, and so on.
raw_text.response: okay determine the seating capacity of a lecture hall if there are 32 rows of seats with 18 seats in the first row 21 in the second row and so on so this is an arithmetic progression and a is 18 The formula is n by 2 into 2a plus n minus 1 into d. d is 3 and n is 32. So 16 into 18 twos are 36 plus 31 into 3 right. yes uh so 31 threes are 93 plus 36 into uh 16 so that is six uh nine nine plus three is 12 129 into 16 which will be 9 6 are 54 5 carried over 6 twos are 12 plus 5 is 17 6 ones are 6 7 0 and 129 now adding 4 9 plus 7 is 16 7 plus 3 is 10 2 0 6 4 is the answer
